title: chassalia subcoriacea
intro: Chassalia subcoriacea, also known as the wild orange or wild orange bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Chassalia subcoriacea can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal remedies such as tinctures or salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
